body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

(Re-evaluation of the 2026-05-18 assessment. Verdict unchanged: yes/yes. The reasoning is reaffirmed against the current adapter source, and the prior assessment's PID-race caveat is REVISED — the source no longer matches the heuristic it described.)

## Reasoning

Claude Code supports launching multiple concurrent CLI sessions in the same cwd as-shipped. Each `claude` invocation mints (or accepts via `--session-id`) a fresh UUID and writes to its own transcript at `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<UUID>.jsonl`. Two sessions in the same cwd produce two distinct transcripts because the path is keyed by UUID, not cwd.

irrlicht observes both sessions via the standard transport. `agent.go` declares `Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.claude/projects/, Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}}`. The fswatcher on `~/.claude/projects/` picks up two new files (one per UUID); the JSONL tailer instantiates a FRESH stateful `Parser{}` per file via `NewParser`, so the two sessions never share parser state. The process scanner uses `ExactName{Name: "claude"}` (pgrep -x), which returns every running `claude` PID; `DiscoverPID` in `pid.go` reconciles transcript ↔ PID and ties each session row to its own PID.

Spec bullets map cleanly:
- 'Two distinct session rows appear, each with its own session_id and PID' — two transcripts → two SessionState rows; each row's PID comes from independent DiscoverPID resolution keyed on the transcript's UUID.
- 'Each session transitions state independently — no flapping between rows' — the tailer is per-file (one goroutine, one Parser) so a turn on session A's transcript only updates session A's metrics. There is no shared-cwd state on the session domain object.
- 'PID-to-session binding is stable and does not cross-contaminate' — once `DiscoverPID` binds, the binding persists until process_exited; the dispatcher keys per-session, not per-cwd. The current DiscoverPID actively defends this invariant (see below).

## PID discovery has been HARDENED — the prior race caveat no longer applies

The 2026-05-18 assessment described DiscoverPID as a 'cwd+start-time heuristic' that 'can briefly swap PID-to-session assignment when two sessions start within the same scanner tick.' Reading the current `pid.go`, that description is no longer accurate (and, by commit dates, was already stale when written):

- **Layer 1 (authoritative):** Claude writes `~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json` containing `{pid, sessionId, cwd}` for every live process. DiscoverPID scans this directory and, on an exact `sessionId == wantSessionID` match (derived from the transcript filename), returns that PID directly — no cwd or start-time reasoning at all. This is the normal path once metadata exists.
- **Layer 2 (restricted cwd fallback):** Used only during the brief startup window before a session's metadata file appears. It first excludes PIDs already claimed by OTHER sessions' metadata (`claimedByOthers`), then binds only if exactly one candidate remains; if two live `claude` processes share the cwd with no disambiguating metadata, it returns 0 so `DiscoverPIDWithRetry` retries — it never guesses. The caller's 'prefer unclaimed else highest PID' disambiguator is deliberately ignored precisely because that strategy let a new session steal a rival's PID (issue #109).
- There is NO start-time logic anywhere — the only `startedAt` reference is a comment documenting the on-disk schema; `claudeSessionMeta` reads only `pid` and `sessionId`.

This hardening landed in #109 (2026-04-09) and #169 (2026-04-19), both BEFORE the 2026-05-18 assessment. The two-sessions-same-tick swap the old caveat warned about cannot occur on the metadata path, and on the fallback path the daemon returns 0-and-retries rather than mis-binding. The residual seam is benign: a session may sit at PID=0 for a tick or two until either its metadata file lands or the rival's metadata disambiguates it — observable only as a momentarily-late PID, never as a cross-contaminated binding.

## Status of this cell

The verdict is unchanged (yes/yes, confidence 0.9) and the matrix already records yes/yes. The cell's real blocker is NOT an assessment gap — it is Stage 4 (recording): the catalog shows `measurement.status: no_recording`, recipe `authored: false`, `archive_count: 0`. The next action to finish this cell is recipe + record, not re-assessment.
caveats: Recording recipe must launch two `claude` processes in the same cwd with distinct `--session-id` UUIDs and drive them in parallel (tmux split windows or two background sends). A naive single-shell recipe will serialize the turns.

During the brief startup window before a session's ~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json metadata appears, two concurrent sessions in the same cwd may each sit at PID=0 for a tick or two while DiscoverPID returns 0-and-retries rather than guess. This is observable as a momentarily-late PID, never as a swapped/cross-contaminated binding. Anchor PID-to-session phase checks at a moment after both transcripts have written at least one line.

DiscoverPID hardcodes ~/.claude/sessions/ for the authoritative metadata lookup and does not follow CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (per the comment in adapter.go). A recipe that relocates the config root via that env var would fall back to the Layer-2 cwd path and could leave concurrent same-cwd sessions at PID=0 longer; record under the default config root.

sources.note: Source: FilesUnderRoot{Dir: transcriptsDir(), Parser: JSONLineParser{NewParser: () => &Parser{}}} — a fresh stateful Parser per transcript file means two sessions in one cwd do not share parser state; Process.Match = ExactName{ProcessName "claude"} (pgrep -x) returns every live claude PID.

DiscoverPID is now metadata-first: Layer 1 returns the PID whose ~/.claude/sessions/<pid>.json sessionId exactly matches the transcript UUID; Layer 2 cwd fallback excludes PIDs claimed by other sessions and returns 0 (retry) when ambiguous rather than guessing. No start-time heuristic exists. Hardened in #109 and #169, both predating the 2026-05-18 assessment.
narrowByCWD / DiscoverPIDByCWD: the underlying cwd matcher. claudecode wraps its disambiguate callback to enforce the strict unambiguous-or-zero rule rather than the default highest-PID pick.

Commit history on pid.go: #109 (fa6180c, 2026-04-09) introduced ~/.claude/sessions metadata-based PID discovery to fix the flap loop; #169 (0012099, 2026-04-19) gated the negative filter on metadata mtime; e3cc0b9 (PR #174) tightened review findings. All predate 2026-05-18.

### multiple-sessions-same-cwd — Primitive exercised: 'PID-based session isolation — daemon correctly binds each session to its own PID even when multiple agents share the same transcript directory and filename pattern.' Maps to capabilities.json key session_id_assignment (true). User-observable signal: two distinct session rows with separate session_ids and PIDs, independent transitions, no flapping.
